title: How a fresh project gets the examples
question: How should a new project actually receive the example decisions — by cloning this repo, or by the skill creating them?
rationale: Installing is already 'copy the skill into Claude Code and run it in your own project' — not 'clone this repo'. So the skill scaffolds the three examples the first time it runs in a project with no decisions yet, and tells the user they're samples to edit or delete. The cloner never inherits the skill source, git history, or demo wiring they'd have to strip out, and it fits the choice to keep the real log on its own branch (d56) — a half-template default branch would only confuse.

options.label: Clone this repo as the template

tradeoffs.note: no new code — just trim the folder
inherits skill source, history, and demo config to gut by hand
install is 'copy the skill', not 'clone the repo'

options.label: Skill scaffolds the examples on first run

tradeoffs.note: a small first-run step in the skill that copies the samples in
examples land in their own project; nothing extra inherited
matches the existing 'copy the skill, run it in your project' model
two copies of the samples to keep in step with the schema
covers either entry point, but more to maintain
supports cloning too, which the install model doesn't use
